assertions.description: Calls graph.on('nodeAdded', ...) with correct event name
Calls graph.on('edgeAdded', ...) or graph.on('directedEdgeAdded', ...) with correct event name
Calls graph.on('nodeDropped', ...) with correct event name
assertions.evidence: graph.on('nodeDropped', ({ key }) => { adjMap.delete(key); for (const neighbors of adjMap.values()) neighbors.delete(key); })

assertions.description: Calls graph.on('edgeDropped', ...) or similar with correct event name
adjMap is updated inside event handlers (not just imperatively)
assertions.evidence: All adjMap mutations are inside graph.on() callbacks

assertions.description: Calls graph.dropNode('c') to trigger cascading event handlers

qualitative_notes: Uses addDirectedEdge instead of addEdge on a DirectedGraph (redundant but harmless). Otherwise identical logic to with_skill.
